_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe 3D positions using a three-dimensional coordinate system.

_source.w: We are learning to describe 3D positions using a three-dimensional coordinate system.
y: Three-dimensional coordinates extend our spatial skills from the flat plane into the space around us.
3D coordinate systems are used in architecture, aviation (latitude, longitude, altitude), medical imaging and gaming.
This prepares us for understanding vectors and 3D modelling in later mathematics.
eg: Plotting the point (3, 2, 4) in a 3D coordinate system.
Describing the position of a point in a room using (length, width, height) coordinates.
Finding the distance between two points in 3D space using an extension of Pythagoras' theorem.
Describing the vertices of a rectangular prism using 3D coordinates.
Connecting 3D coordinates to real-world applications like GPS (latitude, longitude, elevation).
Representing the flight path of an aircraft using 3D coordinates over time.
